requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Flight software that is modifiable during flight shall be protected from unintended modifications including those caused by operations errors, single event effects, and hardware problems. 2
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Modification Protection Modifiable flight software shall be protected from unintended modifications from errors, radiation, or hardware issues.
:
requirements.comments:
Version 1: whenever modification_during_flight flight_software shall immediately satisfy no_unintended_modifications.

Version 2: flight_software shall always satisfy (errors | radiation | hardware_issues) -> flight_software_protected
requirements.fulltext: flight_software shall always satisfy (errors | radiation | hardware_issues) -> flight_software_protected

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, (( errors | radiation | hardware_issues ) -> flight_software_protected) must hold at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, (( errors | radiation | hardware_issues ) -> flight_software_protected) must hold at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.
semantics.CoCoSpecCode: H((((errors or radiation) or hardware_issues) => flight_software_protected))

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Flight software shall be designed to send acknowledgement of command receipt to the source with indication of acceptance or rejection of command. 4
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Flight software shall acknowledge command receipt with acceptance or rejection status.
:
requirements.comments:
What should the timing be? eventually, at the next timepoint, within some duration?

Potentially make acceptance_status variable name more clear/descriptive

Version 1: whenever command_receipt flight_software shall at the next timepoint satisfy send_acknowledgement_message_to_source & (send_command_accepted_to_source | (send_command_rejected_to_source & acknowledgement_message_includes_rejection_reason) ).
requirements.fulltext: whenever command_receipt flight_software shall immediately satisfy acknowledge_receipt & acceptance_status.

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (command_receipt) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, if trigger holds then (acknowledge_receipt & acceptance_status) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (command_receipt) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, if trigger holds then (acknowledge_receipt & acceptance_status) also holds at the same time point.

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: The requirements of this section are intended for newly developed code. 1
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Applicability The requirements in this section shall apply to newly developed code.
:
requirements.comments: This is the parent requirement for STD8070.1-004 - STD8070.1-034
requirements.fulltext: "The requirements in this section shall apply to newly developed code."

semantics.description: FRET only speaks FRETish but as a courtesy will save this requirement.

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: The flight software design shall (design guidance for Class D) provide the capability to upload new software and replace old modules during the mission. 2
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Software Modifiability Flight software design shall provide the capability to upload new software and replace modules during the mission.
:
requirements.fulltext: whenever mission_active, flight_software shall always satisfy allow_new_software_uploads & allow_module_replacements.

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (mission_active) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, (allow_new_software_uploads & allow_module_replacements) must hold at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (mission_active) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, (allow_new_software_uploads & allow_module_replacements) must hold at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Flight software shall be designed to initialize itself and any associated hardware, including any back-up hardware or software system, to a safe and known state upon startup. 3
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Startup Initialization Flight software shall initialize itself and all associated hardware (including backups) to a safe, known state upon startup.
:
requirements.fulltext: upon state_is_STARTUP, the flight_software shall immediately satisfy software_initialized & hardware_initialized & backup_hardware_initialized

SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval if (state_is_STARTUP) is true and any point in the interval where (state_is_STARTUP) becomes true (from false).
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, if trigger holds then (software_initialized & hardware_initialized & backup_hardware_initialized) also holds at the same time point.

SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval if (state_is_STARTUP) is true and any point in the interval where (state_is_STARTUP) becomes true (from false).
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, if trigger holds then (software_initialized & hardware_initialized & backup_hardware_initialized) also holds at the same time point.

semantics.CoCoSpecCode: H(((state_is_STARTUP and ZtoPre(not (state_is_STARTUP))) => ((software_initialized and hardware_initialized) and backup_hardware_initialized)))

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Software shall accommodate both nominal inputs (within specifications) and off-nominal inputs, from which recovery may be required. 7
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Input Accommodation Software shall accommodate both nominal and off-nominal inputs, including recovery from the latter.
:
requirements.fulltext: "Software shall accommodate both nominal and off-nominal inputs, including recovery from the latter."

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Software design documentation contents shall comply with the documentation requirements of APR 7150.2. 1
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Documentation Standards Software design documentation contents shall meet APR 7150.2 documentation requirements.
:
requirements.fulltext: flight_software shall always satisfy software_design_documentation_follows_apr7150.2_requirements.

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: If not removed, the test capabilities do not cause flight hardware damage or interfere with proper operation of the flight software if inadvertently executed in flight. 9
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Diagnostic Safety Resident test capabilities shall not cause damage or interfere with flight operations if inadvertently triggered.
:
requirements.fulltext: whenever state_is_FLIGHT & operational_testing_capabilities_enabled & operational_testing_capabilities_triggered flight_software shall immediately satisfy !damage_hardware & !interfere_with_flight_operations.

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (state_is_FLIGHT & operational_testing_capabilities_enabled & operational_testing_capabilities_triggered) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, if trigger holds then (! damage_hardware & ! interfere_with_flight_operations) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (state_is_FLIGHT & operational_testing_capabilities_enabled & operational_testing_capabilities_triggered) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, if trigger holds then (! damage_hardware & ! interfere_with_flight_operations) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.CoCoSpecCode: H((((state_is_FLIGHT and operational_testing_capabilities_enabled) and operational_testing_capabilities_triggered) => (not (damage_hardware) and not (interfere_with_flight_operations))))

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Flight software shall reject, and log incorrectly formatted commands, data, or loads and provide notification that they were incorrectly formatted.
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Command Rejection Flight software shall reject incorrectly formatted commands, data, or loads.

Important Example

Version 1: flight_software shall immediately satisfy (incorrectly_formatted_command -> reject_command & provide_incorrectly_formatted_commands_notification) & (incorrectly_formatted_data -> reject_data & provide_incorrectly_formatted_data_notification) & (incorrectly_formatted_load -> reject_load & provide_incorrectly_formatted_load_notification).
requirements.fulltext: whenever (command_incorrectly_formatted | data_incorrectly_formatted | load_incorrectly_formatted), the flight_software shall immediately satisfy ((command_incorrectly_formatted -> (reject_command & log_incorrect_format & provide_notification)) & (data_incorrectly_formatted -> (reject_data & log_incorrect_format & provide_notification)) & (load_incorrectly_formatted -> (reject_load & log_incorrect_format & provide_notification)))

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Where commercial hardware, operating systems, or other tools are used to support testing, flight software design should accommodate such platforms with minor change, such that these tests are relevant to software V&V. 2
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: COTS Compatibility Flight software design should accommodate commercial platforms for testing with minor changes to ensure V&V relevance.
:
requirements.fulltext: the flight_software shall always satisfy flight_software_design_accommodates_commercial_platforms

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, (flight_software_design_accommodates_commercial_platforms) must hold at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, (flight_software_design_accommodates_commercial_platforms) must hold at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.

requirements.rationale:
Software shall be designed to prevent incorrect use of memory with the following considerations: a. Execution in data areas, unused areas, and areas not intended for execution. 7, 8

Memory Execution Protection Software design shall prevent execution in data, unused, or unintended memory areas.
requirements.fulltext: whenever in_data_memory | in_unused_memory | in_unintended_memory flight_software shall immediately satisfy prevent_execution

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (in_data_memory | in_unused_memory | in_unintended_memory) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, if trigger holds then (prevent_execution) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (in_data_memory | in_unused_memory | in_unintended_memory) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, if trigger holds then (prevent_execution) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.CoCoSpecCode: H((((in_data_memory or in_unused_memory) or in_unintended_memory) => prevent_execution))

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Software stressed beyond limits should be precluding an undesired response to mathematical singularities or limitations. 5
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Singularity Protection Software should preclude undesired responses to mathematical singularities or limitations.
:
requirements.fulltext: whenever performance_limits_exceeded & (mathematical_singularity_present | mathematical_limitation) the flight_software shall never satisfy undesired_response.

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (performance_limits_exceeded & ( mathematical_singularity_present | mathematical_limitation )) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, (undesired_response) must be false at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (performance_limits_exceeded & ( mathematical_singularity_present | mathematical_limitation )) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, (undesired_response) must be false at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.
semantics.CoCoSpecCode: H((H(not ((performance_limits_exceeded and (mathematical_singularity_present or mathematical_limitation)))) or not (undesired_response)))

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: The spacecraft data system shall accommodate flight operational uncertainties caused by weather effects or ground tracking station problems. 9
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Operational Uncertainty Resilience The data system shall accommodate operational uncertainties from weather or ground station issues.
:
requirements.fulltext: whenever weather_issues | ground_station_issues spacecraft_data_system shall immediately satisfy operational_uncertainties_accommodated

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (weather_issues | ground_station_issues) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, if trigger holds then (operational_uncertainties_accommodated) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (weather_issues | ground_station_issues) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, if trigger holds then (operational_uncertainties_accommodated) also holds at the same time point.

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Software stressed beyond limits should be sensitive to identified uncertainties. 5
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Uncertainty Sensitivity Software should be sensitive to identified uncertainties when stressed.
:
requirements.fulltext: whenever performance_limits_exceeded, the flight_software shall immediately satisfy sensitive_to_uncertainties.

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (performance_limits_exceeded) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, if trigger holds then (sensitive_to_uncertainties) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (performance_limits_exceeded) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, if trigger holds then (sensitive_to_uncertainties) also holds at the same time point.

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Flight software shall be designed to verify uplinked commands, data, or loads. 3
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Uplink Verification Flight software shall be designed to verify all uplinked commands, data, or loads.
:
requirements.fulltext: whenever uplinked_command | uplinked_data | uplinked_load the flight_software shall at the next timepoint satisfy uplink_verified

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (uplinked_command | uplinked_data | uplinked_load) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, (uplink_verified) must hold at the next time step.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (uplinked_command | uplinked_data | uplinked_load) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, (uplink_verified) must hold at the next time step.
semantics.CoCoSpecCode: H((YtoPre(((uplinked_command or uplinked_data) or uplinked_load)) => (uplink_verified or ZtoPre(false))))

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Flight software shall be designed to detect and respond to memory faults allocated to the software, such as stuck bits or single event upsets (SEU) if architecture is not protected in hardware (e.g., Triple Modular Redundancy (TMR)). 4
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Radiation Response Flight software shall detect and respond to software-allocated memory faults if hardware protection is absent.
:
requirements.comments: if hardware_protection_absent flight_software shall always satisfy (software_detects_memory_faults & software_handles_memory_faults).
requirements.fulltext: whenever !hardware_protection upon software_allocated_memory_fault the flight_software shall immediately satisfy detects_memory_fault & responds_to_memory_fault

semantics.description:

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: The software shall be designed to detect off-nominal restarts and to successively
reinitialize with less and less dependency on preserved state (e.g. inertial,
temporal, device capability or configuration, file allocation tables, boot code in
RAM, etc.) from before the most recent reset, until a fully known and tested initial
configuration is obtained, and until stable operation has been restored.

REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Restart Detection Software shall be designed to detect off-nominal restarts.
:
requirements.comments: the flight_software shall until stable_known_configuration_reached & stable_operation_restored, satisfy reinitialize_with_decreasing_dependency_on_preserved_state.
requirements.fulltext: the flight_software shall until stable_known_configuration_reached & stable_operation_restored, satisfy reinitialize_with_decreasing_dependency_on_preserved_state.

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, (reinitialize_with_decreasing_dependency_on_preserved_state) must remain true until (but not necessarily including) the point where the stop condition holds, or to the end of the interval. If the stop condition never occurs, (reinitialize_with_decreasing_dependency_on_preserved_state) must hold until the end of the scope, or forever. If the stop condition holds at the trigger, the requirement is satisfied.
semantics.probabilistic_description:

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (data_passed_between_processes | parameter_lists_passed_between_processses) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, if trigger holds then (validate_temporal_consistency) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (data_passed_between_processes | parameter_lists_passed_between_processses) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, if trigger holds then (validate_temporal_consistency) also holds at the same time point.

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: Software shall be specified and designed to maintain the aliveness and continuity of the software when failed input/output transactions or other processing or interactions fail to complete. 6
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: I/O Failure Continuity Software shall maintain aliveness and continuity when I/O transactions or processing fail to complete.
:
requirements.comments: STD8070.1-022 uses continue_functioning. I chose to use continue_functioning to represent 'maintain aliveness and continuity" becuase i believe they mean the same thing
requirements.fulltext: whenever io_transactions_failed | processing_failed flight_software shall immediately satisfy continue_functioning

semantics.description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (io_transactions_failed | processing_failed) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, if trigger holds then (continue_functioning) also holds at the same time point.
semantics.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval defined by the entire execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where (io_transactions_failed | processing_failed) is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, if trigger holds then (continue_functioning) also holds at the same time point.

requirements.rationale:
VERBATIM TEXT FROM SOURCE: The action to be taken in such situations should be specified as part of the requirements on the design. 5
REQUIREMENT DESCRIPTION: Oversubscription Specification Actions for resource over-subscription shall be specified in the design requirements.
:
requirements.fulltext: "Oversubscription Specification Actions for resource over-subscription shall be specified in the design requirements."
